lines.target: We see Henderson Dores walking up Park Avenue in New York — he is late, he is thinking; and he
lines.base: Observamos a Henderson Dores que sube por Park Avenue en Nueva York — está retrasado, piensa; y

lines.target: brings his sabres in a thin bag slung over his right shoulder,
lines.base: lleva sus sables en una bolsa delgada colgada sobre su hombro derecho,

lines.target: and tries to look calm and at ease, but
lines.base: y trata de parecer calmado y tranquilo, pero

lines.target: that permanently worried expression on his square open face gives him rather away.
lines.base: esa expresión constantemente preocupada en su rostro cuadrado y abierto le delata un poco.

lines.target: The crowds of Americans — clean and well-dressed — stride past him purposefully, unheeding, confident.
lines.base: Las multitudes de americanos — limpios y bien vestidos — pasan junto a él a grandes pasos, con determinación, sin prestarle atención, confiados.

lines.target: Henderson walks on. He is nearly forty years old — his birthday is coming up — and just under six feet tall.
lines.base: Henderson continua andando. Tiene casi cuarenta años — su aniversario está al caer — y mide un poco menos de seis pies de altura.

lines.target: His frame is sturdy and his face is pleasantly attractive. To his surprise, people are inclined to like him on first acquaintance.
lines.base: Su complexión es sólida y su cara generosa y agradablemente atractiva. Siempre le sorprende que las gentes se inclinen a simpatizar con él desde el primer encuentro.

lines.target: He is polite, quite smartly dressed, apart from that slight frown on his forehead:
lines.base: Es cortés, va bastante bien vestido, y, aparte de ese ligero fruncimiento del ceño,

lines.target: he seems as composed and as unconcerned as, well, as you or me.
lines.base: parece tan sereno y tan despreocupado como... como lo podemos estar usted y yo. Pero a Henderson le aqueja un descontento, un rencor, un ruñido de naturaleza profunda e insidiosa.

lines.target: Henderson has a complaint, a grumble of a deep and insidious nature.
lines.base: Ha dejado de estimarse; no está contento con la personalidad que se le ha adjudicado, ¡mil gracias!

lines.target: He doesn't like himself any more. He's not happy with the personality he's been provided with, thank you very much.
lines.base: Existe algo dentro de él que no está a la altura, que no le satisface. Se quedará con la envoltura material, ahora bien le gustaría llegar a un pacto respecto al espíritu, si no hay inconveniente.

lines.target: Something about him isn't up to scratch, won't do. He'll keep the flesh, but he'd like to do a deal on the spirit, if you've no objections.
lines.base: Para un observador experimentado, en efecto, todo en él proclama su condición de inglés. Su corte de pelo — ya descrito —

lines.target: To the practised observer, everything about him proclaims his Englishness. His haircut — already noted —
lines.base: sus ojos bordeados de pestañas claras, el vello sobre sus mejillas sin afeitar, su traje azul con dos aberturas en el chaleco,

lines.target: his pale-lashed eyes, the bloom on his unshaven cheekbones, his blue suit with its double vents in the jacket,
lines.base: la sortija de oro mate y usada en el tercer dedo de la mano izquierda, sus calcetines a media pierna de color azul marino (sólo los mayordomos y los choferes llevan negros),

lines.target: the dull worn signet ring on his finger of his left hand, his ankle-length socks (only butlers and chauffeurs wear black),
lines.base: y sus zapatos «Oxford» brillantes y arrugados, con puntera reforzada.

lines.target: and his shiny, well-creased, toe-capped Oxford shoes.
lines.base: Este conocimiento — que es tan fácil de reconocer — le perturbaría, debido a que en efecto, su único anhelo

lines.target: This knowledge — that he is so distinguishable — would distress him, in fact, his grand and only wish
lines.base: es el de encajar, fundirse y mezclarse con la identidad de estas gentes honestas y envidiables que van a trabajar.

lines.target: is to fit in; to merge and blend with the earnest, enviable people all around him on their way to work.
lines.base: Justamente ser otro habitante cualquiera de Manhattan, se dice a sí mismo, cambiándose sus sables al hombro izquierdo, como todo el mundo aquí.

lines.target: Just another Manhattanite, he tells himself, as he transfers his sabres to his left shoulder, just like everybody else here.
lines.base: De nuevo frunce el ceño ligeramente y acorta el paso. Adora a América, pero ¿acaso América le corresponderá? Más allá, el lunático le acecha.

lines.target: He frowns again slightly and shortens his step. He loves America, but will America love him back? Up ahead, the lunatic awaits.

gap_candidates.target: brings his sabres in a thin bag slung over his right shoulder,
and tries to look calm and at ease, but
that permanently worried expression on his square open face gives him rather away.
The crowds of Americans — clean and well-dressed — stride past him purposefully, unheeding, confident.
His frame is sturdy and his face is pleasantly attractive. To his surprise, people are inclined to like him on first acquaintance.
He is polite, quite smartly dressed, apart from that slight frown on his forehead:
Something about him isn't up to scratch, won't do. He'll keep the flesh, but he'd like to do a deal on the spirit, if you've no objections.
his pale-lashed eyes, the bloom on his unshaven cheekbones, his blue suit with its double vents in the jacket,
the dull worn signet ring on his finger of his left hand, his ankle-length socks (only butlers and chauffeurs wear black),
and his shiny, well-creased, toe-capped Oxford shoes.
Just another Manhattanite, he tells himself, as he transfers his sabres to his left shoulder, just like everybody else here.
He frowns again slightly and shortens his step. He loves America, but will America love him back? Up ahead, the lunatic awaits.
